arguments.name: explore-aot-issues
arguments.prompt:
Explore the C# project in /tmp/sv-d565232bfb584ac7bae618f1d90f2376/before/ thoroughly and identify all patterns that are incompatible with .NET Native AOT compilation.

Key things to search for:
1. Use of `Activator.CreateInstance` with generic type parameters or runtime-resolved types
2. Reflection-based serialization/deserialization (especially System.Text.Json without source generators, or JSON converters using reflection)
3. `Type.MakeGenericType`, `Type.GetType`, or runtime type resolution
4. Dynamic assembly loading or `Assembly.Load`
5. Expression tree compilation (`Expression.Compile()`)
6. `MakeGenericMethod` calls
7. Any `[DynamicallyAccessedMembers]` or `[RequiresUnreferencedCode]` attributes already present (which indicate awareness of trimming issues)
8. Look at all serialization-related code (JSON serializers, converters, model reader/writer patterns)
9. Check if there's a `JsonSerializerContext` or source-generated serializers
10. Check for `IUtf8JsonSerializable` and how serialization/deserialization works
11. Look at `GenericOperationSource.cs`, `ArmOperation.cs`, `ArmOperationOfT.cs`, `RehydrationOperation.cs`, `RehydrationOperationOfT.cs` for reflection patterns
12. Look at `NextLinkOperationImplementation.cs` for any dynamic dispatch
13. Look at `ManagedServiceIdentityTypeV3Converter.cs` for JSON converter patterns
14. Check all files in Common/, Shared/, Extensions/, Resources/, ManagementGroup/ directories
